rules.name: Type Scale
rules.description: Use a consistent typographic scale for heading and body sizes.
rules.application: Choose a scale ratio (1.25, 1.333, etc.) and derive all sizes from it.
rules.example: Base 16px with headings at 20, 25, 32, 40, 50px using a 1.25 ratio.
rules.anti_pattern: Arbitrary font sizes like 13px, 17px, 23px with no mathematical relationship.

rules.name: Line Height
rules.description: Set line height between 1.4 and 1.8 for body text readability.
rules.application: Use 1.5 line height for body text, tighter (1.1-1.2) for headings.
rules.example: Body text at 16px/24px (1.5 line height) for comfortable reading.
rules.anti_pattern: Single-spaced body text (line-height: 1.0) making paragraphs hard to read.

rules.name: Font Pairing
rules.description: Limit the design to 2-3 font families maximum.
rules.application: Pair a sans-serif for UI with a serif or different sans for headings.
rules.example: Inter for body and UI, plus a display font for hero headings.
rules.anti_pattern: Using 5+ different font families creating visual chaos.

rules.name: Measure (Line Length)
rules.description: Keep line length between 45-75 characters for optimal readability.
rules.application: Constrain text containers to max-width 65ch for body content.
rules.example: A blog post with content constrained to 680px width.
rules.anti_pattern: Full-width text spanning 1440px making lines extremely long.

rules.name: Font Weight Hierarchy
rules.description: Use font weight to reinforce the visual hierarchy.
rules.application: Bold (700) for headings, medium (500) for emphasis, regular (400) for body.
rules.example: Section title in bold, subheading in medium, body in regular weight.
rules.anti_pattern: Everything in bold or everything in regular, losing hierarchy cues.

rules.name: Responsive Typography
rules.description: Adjust font sizes for different viewport sizes.
rules.application: Use smaller base sizes on mobile, larger on desktop.
rules.example: H1 at 32px on mobile scaling to 56px on desktop.
rules.anti_pattern: Using the same 48px heading on mobile causing text to overflow.
